Module 4:
Creating Prelaunch Content,
Part 2
!
Executive Summary
!
• 	Screencap video vs. full motion video
• 	Screen capture is often easier than full motion for many reasons - lots of
logistics and technical pieces with full motion
• 	It can be a lot easier to deliver a script with a screen cap video
• 	If this is your first launch or you're just starting out you may want to script
your video tightly
• 	If you're going to deliver from a script with a screen capture you could
read it off your computer screen
• 	With screencap you can record the audio first, and then you do the on-
screen video recording after
• 	A lot of people dislike being on camera and have many worries - just
practice getting in front of the camera
• 	If that’s the case and you can’t get around it - screencap is the way
to go
• 	It's generally easier for the viewer to connect with you if it’s full motion
video
• 	It's harder to connect with someone if it's a screen capture video
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 2 --

• 	If you had never seen me in full motion, you just wouldn't have as close a
connection with me as you do
• 	If you need to edit something into a full motion video, you can actually
record it as a screencap and put a little section in the middle of your full
motion video
• 	Screen capture can be a lot faster to create
• 	But if you want to use fancy animations it does get slower and it's a
lot more effort to create
• 	Important: you can mix full motion with screen capture video - I’ve done
this a lot for my launches
!
